title: Kaworu XX Speculative
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu XX [Speculative]             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Nov 07, 2006 8:00 pm Split from New Gainax figures - Angels in female human form . --Reichu Reichu wrote: Sailor Star Dust wrote: Although I first I was rather um, surpised to see Kaworu aka Tabiris in female form, I guess under the circumstances (Adam= female and Adam's soul and all), it makes sense, huh?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Nov 07, 2006 8:13 pm Um, could you just sum up in a nutshell just what Reichu was getting at? I have no time to read all those pages of the discussion. :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Nov 07, 2006 8:15 pm Um in simple terms Kaworu is a girl taking her appearence from the FAR thus why in Lilim terms she doesn't look female.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Nov 07, 2006 8:15 pm FAR?? :? Like I said before, proof that Shinji's straight after all! :mrgreen:

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Nov 07, 2006 8:19 pm First Ancestor Race: 18. First Ancestral Race A. Publicly Released Information Data unavailable. B. Generally Recognized Information The entities thought to have created the Angels. The specifics of their intention are unclear. C. Confidential Information They were the first extraterrestrial intelligence. The humanoid species, referred to as the First Ancestral Race, started to spread Seeds of Life throughout the Milky Way Galaxy. As of yet, we do not know their motives or for what they were aiming. It is becoming evident that multiple Seeds were disseminated. Eventually, by force of sheer bad luck, two accidentally landed on the same planet: Adam of the White Moon, and Lilith of the Black Moon. D. In-Depth Information Within a carrier known as a "Moon", the First Ancestral Race fabricated a perfect cavity (also a moon), at which point the Seed, or "Progenitor Entity", would be placed inside and sent out into space. That was their technology, and, from the perspective of Angels, humans, and others, they might be called gods.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 07, 2006 9:46 pm At last count, only four known individuals actually endorse "that" theory. The reasoning behind the theory is a bit... tangled, but it sorta goes like this: The FAR were a non-mammalian species of human lacking the marked sexual dimorphism present in our own -- that is, the only notable differences between male and female were primary sexual characteristics, those involved directly in reproduction. Adam is, physically, a "god"-version of a FAR individual and a female specialized for Seed duty. Lilim genes "dived" into Adam and physically fused into one of her eggs, producing a bona fide immaculate conception. The result was the hybrid entity, Tabris/Kaworu, half Adam, half you-know-who. Kaworu is, overall, molded in the image of the father. Adam's genes seem to have little influence -- aside from prompting the strange pigmentation, they aren't discernibly doing anything. ...unless one cannot purge certain nagging vibes emanating from the smooth-crotched Kaworu in EoE from the mind, anyway. And somehow associate that with the look Shinji gave Kaworu in the shower and how totally weird that cut has always been. And there is also the musing, "Why would Adam be occupying a male body, anyway?" So eventually comes the whimsy that maybe Kaworu isn't really a guy -- "he" just looks like one, being as "he" has the physical appearance of an adolescent Dr. Dad. But in reality, Kaworu harbors "plumbing" like Adam, and as such approaches maturation from the vantage point that human males are physically much more like FAR females than Lilim females are... So, come puberty, Keel's little girl starts to develop a boyish figure and a deep voice. "Thank heaven for unisex names. Kaworu, you're a boy from now on." "僕が？" "Yes, just like that." "You Lilim are strange." Then, eventually, Shinji is purged of all doubts and he discovers the real truth: "You were on the other side of the river all along, Kaworu-kun?" Can't do much with this in the debate arena, but I think it's very nifty conceptually. It opens up a lot of gates for speculative possibilities. Kaworu's hybrid status is traditionally glossed over (if noted at all). The concept that his soul is female and maternal in nature has its friends and adversaries, but, if considered, adds an extra dimension to things. Take that one step further -- a very big step that goes "over the deep end" -- and contemplate Kaworu as a very unusual young woman, and things get even more interesting... ... harpooned sea nymphs.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Tue Nov 07, 2006 10:05 pm That's an interesting idea, but... how would K-kun keep everyone from noticing? Apart from Shinji in the bath scene and all, wouldn't he have been scanned by the medics at least once? If you can come up with a good explanation, I will seriously look into using that in TToR404.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Nov 07, 2006 10:13 pm I go back an forth on this one. I can see Adam trying to quicken her new body in the form of her old one but do to that pesky Lilim gets Kaworu/Tabris as a result. Adam: Why does the universe hate me so? I don't wanna fight Lilith, that damned spear knocks me out for 4 billion years, when I come to I discover I've been raped, I get blown to kingom come, and now this! You left out the sea shore reference. Well half of it anyways. BobBQ wrote: That's an interesting idea, but... how would K-kun keep everyone from noticing? Apart from Shinji in the bath scene and all, wouldn't he have been scanned by the medics at least once?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 07, 2006 11:37 pm BobBQ wrote: That's an interesting idea, but... how would K-kun keep everyone from noticing? Apart from Shinji in the bath scene and all, wouldn't he have been scanned by the medics at least once?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Nov 08, 2006 12:34 am Doesn't Nagisa mean sea shore or something? Couple that with "women will seem like they're on another shore" line from Kaji. Reichu wrote: RANDOM NERV PERSON: Come with me, Nagisa-san. You need to receive a medical screening. KAWORU: I was screened before I was sent from Germany. You already have the data. RNP: But it's incomplete. We need to-- KAWORU: I'm exempt from further screenings. It wasn't my decision -- it was Seele's. You'll have to contact them, I suppose.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Wed Nov 08, 2006 1:00 am This thread is so ridiculous it's awesome. I proclaim Kaworu to be an animated character, and therefore this speculation is stupid, which means you can go on.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Nov 08, 2006 1:01 am This again? Rachel, kill it with fire if it gets dumb.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Wed Nov 08, 2006 1:03 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Rachel, kill it with fire if it gets dumb.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 08, 2006 1:15 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Doesn't Nagisa mean sea shore or something? Couple that with "women will seem like they're on another shore" line from Kaji.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Nov 08, 2006 1:25 am Blames dub! Reichu wrote: Secrets Down Under or no, it would make sense for Seele to not want Nerv checking out their specimen too closely. The manga doesn't really address this aspect of things, though, does it? Try keeping a secret like being Adam's offspring for that long -- he'd be screwed if anyone decided to sneak a peak at his DNA (before the MAGI did it for them). And if he were scanned by that thing in #04 and came out in pretty rainbow colors a la Rei. Among other possibilities.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Nov 08, 2006 1:31 am But isn't the fusion of Kaworu in Lilith supposed to represent the kabbalistic androgyne?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 08, 2006 1:36 am cat42 wrote: But isn't the fusion of Kaworu in Lilith supposed to represent the kabbalistic androgyne?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Nov 08, 2006 1:40 am EDIT==== Alchemy & Mysticism: The Hermetic Museum by Alexander Roob wrote: "Here, the south wind is represented as a large eagle, gradually uniting the two opposites. The three legs on which the androgynous being stands, refer to the three-footed stand on which the retort is exposed to the fire. After the coupling, Sol says to Luna, 'we will rise into the order of the most ancient [that is 24 sublimations or eagles], then will a burning light be poured into me and you' (Senior Zadith, in: Aurora Consurgens)."

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 08, 2006 1:43 am WTF? Source please, etc. etc.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Nov 08, 2006 1:46 am Reichu wrote: WTF? Source please, etc. etc.

[]: Kaworu XX [Speculative]             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Nov 07, 2006 8:00 pm Split from New Gainax figures - Angels in female human form . --Reichu Reichu wrote: Sailor Star Dust wrote: Although I first I was rather um, surpised to see Kaworu aka Tabiris in female form, I guess under the circumstances (Adam= female and Adam's soul and all), it makes sense, huh?
